results.title: Juno: AI Health Assistant for Chronic Illness | Product Hunt
results.content:
Since launching the app in October:  
  
100,000 downloads  
$75k MRR  
14,000+ five-star reviews  
  
If you, a family member or a friend lives with a chronic illness - please send them Juno. We support a wide range of conditions including fibromyalgia, long COVID, POTS, ME/CFS, EDS, endometriosis, PCOS, lupus, multiple sclerosis - and we would love any feedback.  
  
Thanks for checking us out 🙏 [...] Hey Product Hunt 👋  
  
I'm Isaac, co-founder of Juno.  
  
I grew up with chronic illness and waited 14 years for a diagnosis. My co-founder Marshall lived with ME/CFS through university - an "invisible illness" nobody, including doctors, believed he had. Between us we saw dozens of doctors, repeated our story every visit, and never felt like anyone saw the full picture. That's the reality for over 1 billion people. The average chronic diagnosis takes 7.6 years.  
  
So we built Juno: an AI health companion built specifically for chronic illness.  
You talk to Juno daily, by voice or text - symptoms, sleep, meds, mood. She tracks it automatically, spots patterns, provides personalised advice and turns months of data into a PDF you can actually get through in a 15-minute appointment. [...] Juno

results.title: Juno: Free, local AI powered Voice to Text w/ live transcriptions
results.content:
Jas
Juno

Juno

Hey Product Hunt - Jaski here, developer behind Juno.  
  
Voice is becoming the new keyboard. Juno is the open voice layer for Mac.  
I built it for how I actually work: long prompts, product notes, specs, messages, emails. I wanted to speak naturally, see the transcript live, and get finished writing inside the app I was already using.  
Juno turns messy speech into clean writing, rewrites selected text by voice, and creates Notes, Reminders, and Alarms.   
  
If software hears your voice and understands your screen, it has to be local, open, and unlimited. For this category, open source is not a feature. It is the only acceptable architecture.   
  
Hence, Juno is fully local, open source, and free forever.  
  
Use Juno and share your feedback with me. [...] But users don t feel percentages.

A transcript can be 97% accurate and still feel like garbage if the missing 3% is a client s name, a critical date, a URL, or broken formatting.

For voice input, the real metric is much more binary:

0

3

Jas
Juno

Juno [...] Most dictation tools expect you to speak perfectly, wait, and then clean up the mess afterwards. But real speech is not like that. You pause, restart, correct yourself, say wait, no, mention product names, jump between ideas, and still need the final text to look like something you would actually send.

Juno is our attempt at a different kind of voice tool.

Press a key. Talk naturally. Juno turns the thought into clean text in the app you were already using.

1

4

Juno
Jas

1d ago

### Word Error Rate is Broken: Why Dictation Apps are Testing the Wrong Metric

The standard speech-to-text metric is Word Error Rate, or WER.

But users don t feel percentages.

results.title: Juno digital planner: All your tasks and events in one calm, offline-first planner | Product Hunt
results.content:
Adrián de la Rosa

Juno digital planner

@h1han Thanks for your support! And congrats and good luck on your launch too! I think people like us need this kind of personal planner to reach all of our goals, so I completely see where you're coming from. This project started from our own very real needs, and we use it daily.

I hope Juno is useful for you too!

Alejandro Esquivel
Snowglobe

Snowglobe

Love the focus on keeping that notebook simplicity but making it actually work across devices. The offline-first approach is super refreshing. Congrats on the launch!

Adrián de la Rosa

Juno digital planner

@alejandroesquivel Thank you very much! We very much think offline-first should be a requirement for every planner app. Glad you like the concept!

Rish Praveen
Adrián de la Rosa [...] Esther George
Adrián de la Rosa

Juno digital planner

@george\_esther Exactly! We wanted the focus that a paper notebook gives you, but with all the benefits of a complete software tool. That's why Juno is a private space you control, and where you pull tasks from the integrations but tasks are never pushed to you.

Tasks not done today are automatically moved to tomorrow, and you can see how many days this happened in a row for each task. When this goes over 5 days in a row (configurable), tasks move to the archive. Also, when you plan your day with the morning ritual, we help you estimate time for each task and clearly show how your day looks, warning you if you're overcommitting and helping you reorganize.

Give it a try and let me know how it goes!

Cruise Chen
Agnes AI 

Agnes AI [...] Cruise Chen
Agnes AI 

Agnes AI

Useful for person like me who is always stuck in mess... how painful is the migration and schedule input?

Adrián de la Rosa

Juno digital planner

Jorge Sanz
Adrián de la Rosa

Juno digital planner

Hans Yang

Hi Adrian, our team launches our product same day as Juno. Happy Launching!

For me personally, I always want something like this and help me focus deeper with all my work and personal schedules. Defo would use the product for more and share with friends. Good work!

Adrián de la Rosa

Across the leading medical products, use cases cluster around self-triage, longitudinal tracking, and home-based support. Skinive AI: Skin Scanner, health checkup emphasizes photo-based skin risk checks and mole mapping; Docus.ai centers on symptom and lab interpretation with optional clinician review; AIWatchfulCompanion extends into caregiving with real-time monitoring, alerts, and incident capture for families.
